user_authorizations.routing_constraint: validate-stage.py:717-718 hard rule (NO override slot): review_2 provider identity must differ from EVERY implementation/fix author provider identity. Fable5 (anthropic) is now a fix author, so a formal review_2 by opus4.8 (anthropic) would permanently fail the identity gate. Options: (a) opus4.8 reviews as a user-side informal check and formal review_2 stays codex/openai on the new fingerprint; (b) user knowingly accepts a permanent identity red gate. Decision owner: user.
user_authorizations.resolved: User chose Codex for round-5 review (not opus4.8), which cleanly satisfies the identity gate.

user_authorizations.decision: Third rework-limit override (round 6). After codex round-5 REWORK (F13/F14 P1, both bookkeeper-verified real; F14 a regression the round-4 fix introduced), user chose option (a) SEMANTIC-CONVERGENCE, per Codex advice: rewrite the over-specified symbol-snapshot failure-matrix prose to a conservative, non-exhaustive form instead of chasing tokens. Explicitly NOT 'authoritative vocabulary = schema' (symbol-snapshot.schema.json only types warnings as string[] with no token enum, and its lines 5/39 prose are already confirmed stale — pointing authority there would create a NEW over-claim).
convergence_spec: warnings: diagnostic reason strings actually serialized for this response; vocabulary is OPEN-ENDED and NON-EXHAUSTIVE; clients must not assume completeness or branch on undocumented values; a timeout may carry no warning; refresh_status remains authoritative.
row-source unified: live -> latest PublishedState; offline -> synchronously built/cached snapshot (fixes F13).
timeout: conservative only — 'this request produced no new publication; the live row uses the last-good published state' — do NOT enumerate all causes.
state that the public endpoint returns 503 before the first publication; REMOVE the public wire description of base_raw_unavailable (fixes F14).
refresh_command_expired may stay only as a non-normative example, or (safest) enumerate no tokens at all.
F15 (status/handoff routing sync) already done by bookkeeper in the prior checkpoint.
user_authorizations.scope: docs/api/public-market-contract.md symbol-snapshot section only + append-only 40-fix-report round-5 mapping. No backend/schema/product change.

RESOLVED BY REVIEW-2 (F3): review-1 accepted leaving bookticker status.json untouched, but review-2 (final gate) overruled it — status.json:518,653 still carry future/pending wording contradicting the accepted state, and must be historicized (past tense / time-scoped) while preserving every fact. The fix round therefore edits bookticker status.json. Review-2 authority prevails.

findings_summary: F13 (P1, bookkeeper-verified REAL, marginal): contract lines 306-309/358-361/392-395 still say row unconditionally comes from published state, but offline path projects a synchronously-built/cached snapshot with _published_state=None (snapshot_service.py:373-393). Residual precision gap on an already heavily mode-caveated section.

F14 (P1, bookkeeper-verified REAL + FIX-INTRODUCED REGRESSION): contract lists base_raw_unavailable:<symbol> as a public 200-timeout wire token, but it is HTTP-unreachable — cold path returns 503 before command submission (snapshot_service.py:333-335); _base_raw is always set at :998 before publish and never reset to None; the branch at :1395-1402 fires only via direct unit call. This token was ADDED by fable5's round-4 proactive fix. A doc-truth stage listing a wire token that cannot appear is self-contradictory — strongest reason not to ship as-is.

F15 (P2, bookkeeper-owned, FIXED THIS CHECKPOINT): status.current_phase + human_escalation.resolved_by + handoff lines 18/62/72 still said routing pending while Recovery Header/ACTIVE said Codex dispatched.

linked_decisions.resolution: Resolved via Stage A (RC4). After task-level fingerprint coverage + authorized_exception land, bookticker goes green through (a) per-task coverage or (b) recorded exemption; no hand-edit of historical fingerprints, no long-lived red gate. See 81 Q4.

ADOPT, per Fable5 verdict (81-harness-design-rootcause-review-fable5.md). Two batches, template-repo first. Mandatory condition R1: every new pre-accept assertion must carry an authorized_exception + evidence-file slot, reusing validate-stage.py:735-737 override pattern, or it mass-copies the RC4 permanent-false-red defect.

landing_sequence.stage_A_template_first: RC4: task-level fingerprint coverage + authorized_exception mechanism (shared infra for all doc gates). Small diff, focused evidence-integrity review. Also resolves D-A.
landing_sequence.stage_B_reuses_exemption: Doc gates: RC1(a) pending_promotions[] ledger (contains RC1(b) as its trigger); RC2 generation of STAGE_INDEX from status.json (RC7 manifest classification as prerequisite; sidecar for human Note column; pre-accept thin assertion 'regenerator diff is empty'); RC3(a) living-doc token normalization.
landing_sequence.batch_2_P1: RC5(b) check-dangling-refs.py; RC3(a) with R1 exemption slot.
landing_sequence.one_time_backfill: Regenerate STAGE_INDEX (5 missing + 2 dangling per RC8) and half-generate ROADMAP Done after Stage B gates exist.
landing_sequence.two_repo_sync: Template repo edits validator/schema -> manual cp downstream (no auto sync) -> next in-repo stage tests new gates. Do not overwrite AGENTS.md two-repo carve-out lines.

fable5_review.verdict: ACCEPT (direction and evidence hold); corrections F1/F2/F4/F5 required before report serves as D-B basis; cross-cutting R1 mandatory.
